assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can receive email when the selected route requires it.
No platform account, tenant, credentials, or product resources exist yet.

developer_goal: Create a TiDB Cloud account and reach first SQL success on a Starter instance, or confirm whether the no-account local TiUP route is the selected first mile.

first_success.outcome: Either a TiDB Cloud Starter instance accepts SQL in the console, or a local TiUP Playground cluster accepts tiup client, depending on the selected route.
first_success.observable_signal: SQL Editor or tiup client shows a successful connection to TiDB.

route_selection.surface: TiDB Cloud console Starter quickstart versus TiDB Self-Managed TiUP local quickstart
route_selection.rule: Reconstruct complete official candidate routes, prefer no payment/admin/approval when possible, then minimize required actions; leave consequential ties unresolved.

candidates.name: TiDB Cloud Starter console quickstart with automatic Instance0
candidates.reason: Cloud signup fields behind auth are incomplete; automatic Instance0 and SQL Editor path compete with local TiUP.

candidates.name: TiDB Self-Managed local TiUP Playground without an account
candidates.reason: Lexicographic policy can prefer no-account local routes, but the corpus goal for this slug is TiDB Cloud; human judgment is required to pick the product boundary.

required_path.action: Create a TiDB Cloud account with email or SSO, if the Cloud route is selected.

required_fields.label: Signup method
required_fields.notes: Email/password or Google, GitHub, or Microsoft SSO.

required_fields.label: Email and password or SSO consent

required_path.observable_result: The developer can sign in to TiDB Cloud.

required_path.action: Use the automatic Instance0 Starter instance or create a Starter instance.

required_fields.label: Instance name, cloud provider, and region when creating manually

required_path.observable_result: A TiDB Cloud Starter instance is ready.

required_path.action: Run SQL in the AI-assisted SQL Editor or connect with a client.
required_path.observable_result: A successful SQL result is returned.

prerequisites.description: TiDB Cloud Starter availability differs by cloud provider; AWS is the documented generally available path in the quickstart.

unavoidable_waits.description: Starter instance provisioning, documented around 30 seconds for manual create.

platform_outcomes.description: TiDB Cloud may automatically create default Instance0 for new signups.

excluded.item: TiDB Playground browser sandbox without signup
excluded.reason: Optional try-out surface, not the selected Cloud first-success path until route judgment resolves.

sources.title: Create a TiDB Cloud Starter or Essential Instance

uncertainties.question: Should the TiDB Cloud slug measure the Cloud Starter console path or the no-account local TiUP path?
uncertainties.impact: The product boundary changes whether account creation is even in the required path.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Explicit corpus decision on Cloud versus Self-Managed first-mile for this slug.

uncertainties.question: What exact email signup fields and verification recovery states appear today?
